text.content:
Chapter 3 — Escape from Old Korvosa. The queen's madness blossoms as Kazavon's fragment quickens within her. Quarantined Old Korvosa rots under mob rule — and somewhere in it hides the city's salvation: the missing seneschal, Neolandus Kalepopolis, the one man who can legally depose a corrupt monarch. PCs begin at 8th level → 9th after the Emperor of Old Korvosa → 10th before the Vivified Labyrinth → nearly 11th by chapter's end.

Kazavon's whispers (GM only). For centuries the fangs whispered to sensitive souls, breeding madmen instead of servants. When Ileosa freed the dragon's spirit-fragment, its seven current 'projects' lost their muse — six killed themselves; the seventh is the painter Salvator Scream, Pilts Swastel's gore-artist at Exemplary Execrables.

The Neolandus chain (the chapter's spine): the wounded seneschal fled the Red Mantis to his secret friend Salvator → Salvator hid him with the Arkonas (patron 'Glorio Arkona' — actually the rakshasa Bahor) → the Arkonas now cage Neolandus as an insurance policy against the queen. Vencarlo, investigating, was ambushed by the Red Mantis (they suspect he is Blackjack), survived, tried to infiltrate Arkona Palace — and is now a prisoner alongside the man he sought. The trail the PCs must follow: Vencarlo's home → Salvator → Pilts → the Arkonas.

As the plague breaks, Queen Ileosa — wearing a new crown set with curved fangs — calls a surprise public address. Flanked by Sabina Merrin, the bloatmage seneschal Togomor, Cressida Kroft, and Sable Company commandant Marcus Endrin, she declares victory over blood veil (deftly spinning Davaulus's fate), names the Gray Maidens Korvosa's official protectors with Sabina as general — and disbands the Sable Company, seizing its holdings and demanding Endrin surrender his badge.

The assassination attempt. Endrin instead puts an arrow into the queen — a perfect, fatal shot. Ileosa does not die. The Crown of Fangs knits the wound before the crowd's eyes; she laughs, and the Gray Maidens drag Endrin away (he lives, imprisoned in the Longacre Building — rescuable in Chapter 4). The city reels: their queen cannot be killed. Time this just after Davaulus falls, while the PCs are mopping up the temple — they should hear of it secondhand.

This is the campaign's mid-point reveal: Ileosa is now visibly superhuman, the Gray Maidens are the law, and open opposition is suicide. The Crown of Fangs artifact debuts here; the queen retreats from public view until Chapter 6's endgame begins to turn.

Soon after, a haggard Cressida summons the PCs to a nearly deserted Citadel Volshyenek and, behind a mage's private sanctum, lays it out: Korvosa is being murdered by its queen, and only the PCs can act.

'Korvosa is dying. No, strike that. Korvosa is being murdered. Killed by our queen. The evidence you've uncovered that links her to the plague is damning enough, but now that she's disbanded the Sable Company and reallocated our own funding to the Gray Maidens, she's more in control now than ever. I dare not move against her, as my guards would be executed to the last fighter by her Gray Maidens before sundown. But she must be stopped. And I know of no one else but you to do this deed.

The mission: infiltrate quarantined Old Korvosa, find Vencarlo (start at his home by the Orisini Academy), and trace Neolandus. Cressida warns of the new 'Emperor of Old Korvosa' holding the streets. Ironically the island is the safest place for queen-hunted PCs — if they keep a low profile and avoid Gray Maidens.

Cressida's cache (the citadel's last reserves): 4 potions of cure serious wounds (moderate healing potions), 3 potions of lesser restoration, a wand of invisibility (10 charges), and a wand of cure moderate wounds (30 charges).

Endrin Isle is sealed: the Gray Maidens burned every wooden bridge, walled the stone Jeggare Bridge with a 15-ft wall of stone barricade, and patrol the Narrows in swift barges. Anyone may enter; no one may leave — escapees are warned once, then fired upon.

The four wards: Fort Korvosa (highest, most orderly — the Arkonas' redoubt) · Garrison Hill (burned, scavenger-haunted) · Bridgefront (worst-off, morale shattered) · Old Dock (the most dangerous — the Emperor's domain).

Getting in/out: flight or teleportation trivializes it; otherwise a night swim or skiff across the northern Jeggare (@Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to swim quietly; @Check[type:stealth|dc:18] to evade the barge patrols), returning via the dark, half-burned far side of the Narrows. Don't over-police it — any half-decent plan works.

The mob. Unless the PCs stay unobtrusive, each hour carries a 10% chance (50% per 10 minutes in Old Dock) that mob soldiers notice them — four @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thugs} demand to know their business in 'the emperor's domain.' Color encounters: Tesh Zobberdin (a doomed avenger marching on Old Dock), Olmere Bliversin (a giggling balcony ranter).

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thug (5)}
Pilts's Mob (recurring)Low · 60 XP @ L8

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thugs} — the standard mob shakedown. They scatter once half fall. Repeatable; escalate to 8 thugs (Severe · 120 XP @ L8) if the PCs make a spectacle.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, A — Vencarlo's Home — p.141.

A humble house in southern Fort Korvosa, beside the charred ruin of the Orisini Academy (burned by the Red Mantis after Vencarlo escaped their ambush). The door is unlocked; the rooms are warm, quiet, unlit — and both fireplaces are burning in an 'empty' house.

Reading the scene: @Check[type:perception|dc:20] (in A7/A8; +10 with scent) catches a strange-tanged smoke; @Check[type:crafting|dc:15] identifies residual alchemist's fire, and @Check[type:perception|dc:25] finds it faintly staining walls, floor, and furniture. @Check[type:survival|dc:20]: no real traffic since Vencarlo's last letter; @Check[type:survival|dc:30]: faint marks of a very stealthy recent visitor.

The rooms (A1–A8): a Vudrani-rug entry · a blade-repair workshop · bath · a study of fencing and philosophy texts (the academy's books show business dying after the king's death) · pantry · the living room (lit hearth, hiding-nook under the stairs) · the double-height training room (lit hearth, open rafters) · Vencarlo's unslept-in bedroom.

The ambush. Two @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} squat here in 8-hour shifts (relieved from the guild's hideout below the Longacre Building — a Chapter 4 thread), waiting for Vencarlo. One hides under the A6 stairs, one in the A7 rafters. They pre-buff the moment anyone enters and spring when a PC climbs to A7 — opening by igniting both fireplaces into the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.spreadingFire001]{Spreading House Fire}.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.spreadingFire001]{Spreading House Fire (8)}
Red Mantis ambushSevere · 120 XP @ L8

2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} + the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.spreadingFire001]{fire} they are warded against. A telegraphed Severe: the fire punishes slow play, and the assassins use it as terrain.

The Blackjack cache. @Check[type:perception|dc:30] in the A8 closet reveals a hidden panel; the iron lockbox within (@Check[type:thievery|dc:40], or Hardness 10 / 40 HP — the key is with Vencarlo's confiscated gear in the Arkona vaults, D16) holds a bag of holding (type I) containing Blackjack's full regalia: a black hooded cloak of elvenkind, several masks, 12 masterwork 'B'-pommeled daggers, +1 resilient slick leather armor, boots of elvenkind, an amulet of proof against detection, black gloves of climbing and swimming (two fingers of the right glove false and adjustable — concealing Vencarlo's old dueling wound), and a +2 striking keen rapier. @Check[type:society|dc:15] (automatic for anyone who saw the execution rescue) recognizes Blackjack's signature gear.

The Blackjack legacy. The mantle has passed hero-to-hero for generations; Vencarlo is merely its latest bearer. With this cache, a PC could become the next Blackjack — encourage it; the regalia is sized to fit its wearer. If the PCs don't fight the fire, the house burns (citizens + Arkona brigades contain the blaze but can't save it; the lockbox survives, @Check[type:perception|dc:25] in the rubble).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, The Concerned Student — p.145.

Drawn by the flames or the fight, a familiar face approaches: Amin Jalento (the noble the PCs may have saved from the Chapter 1 mob — or his cousin Gerran, if Amin died), trapped on the island by the quarantine and squatting in a collapsed building across from the academy.

His story: he was Vencarlo's houseguest when Red Mantis assassins stormed the academy — Vencarlo cut one down and fled; the assassins burned the school. Before that, Vencarlo kept strange hours, came home bloodied, and met three times behind closed doors with a wild-haired, paint-stained visitor Amin recognized: the notorious artist Salvator Scream. Their last meeting ended in shouting. Salvator lives at 140 Wave Street, Old Dock — the Emperor's territory.

Story awards: learning of Salvator Scream → 2,400 XP; escorting Amin safely off the island → a further 4,800 XP.

B. The Artist's Lair — Salvator Scream's leaning, decrepit house at 140 Wave Street, Old Dock, near a ruined Narrows bridge. @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15] (gather information) confirms the address and the artist's grisly reputation (Exemplary Execrables' backdrop painter). A locate creature would reveal Salvator is no longer here — the Emperor's thugs took him days ago.

Who's actually home: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori Vaus}, squatting amid her investigation. She is friendly unless attacked — let her be the PCs' strangest handshake of the campaign.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, B1-B2 — p.146.

The smell in this entryway is of must and mildew, much of it coming from the mud tracked over the floorboards, as if a small army had marched through the room. To the south stands a single empty set of shelves.

@Check[type:survival|dc:20] (front room): a half-dozen people tromped through — and a seventh was dragged out, toes scuffing. @Check[type:survival|dc:20] (bedroom): the prints converge on the bed — Salvator was clubbed in his sleep (the blood) and carried off. The trail is days old and leads toward central Old Dock: Pilts's Palace.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, B3 — p.146.

Both of this room's windows are tightly shuttered, yet the air inside seems strangely fresh and scented, no doubt because of the six large candles that burn within. Each candle has been affixed by a glob of melted wax to the crown of a gleaming, polished skull, and each of these impromptu and grisly candleholders has been placed atop an otherwise clear desk to the west, arrayed in a gentle arc. A chair sits before the desk, and a careful stack of papers and scrolls sits inside the arc of skulls. To the south stands a nearly empty cabinet, its shelves barren save for a few paintbrushes and a cracked pottery urn.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori Vaus} works here among the skulls of Kazavon's six suicides (costumer, toymaker, writer, sculptor, tattooist, poet — she names them fondly), questioning the dead with mostly-garbled results.

Her bargain: an alliance to reach Salvator, sealed by her evidence — a bloodstained sleeve-scrap bearing the city's coat of arms, found wedged behind the bed (@Check[type:society|dc:15]: it is from the uniform of the seneschal of Castle Korvosa — torn when Salvator splinted Neolandus's broken arm). She reveals the Emperor of Old Korvosa holds Salvator. Story award for a peaceful alliance. If rebuffed, she cheerfully shadows the party for a dramatic save later.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, B4 — p.147.

This room appears to have once been a combination kitchen and storeroom, but is now a bewildering riot of brightly colored fungus and mold. The stuff grows everywhere and in every color, over tables, across cupboards, and in swaths along the floor and walls, but it seems thickest to the southwest, where puffy sheets of yellow fungus cover several objects so completely it's impossible to make out what lies beneath.

A patch of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.hazards.Actor.A93flWUsot3FmC7t]{Yellow Mold} (the official L8 hazard) chokes the sunless northeast corner — Salvator handled his samples only in sunlight; his abductors didn't know to.

Beneath the growth, among the painting supplies: a full jar of marvelous pigments — the chapter's signature wondrous treasure.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, B5 — p.147.

What once might have been a small house has been obliterated, collapsed from within by a twenty-foot-diameter sinkhole. Water from the Narrows fills the hole, creating a muddy pit strewn with jagged bits of timber and flotsam. The edges of the hole are slick and patchy with fungus. Immediately south of the sinkhole, the boardwalk has also collapsed, creating a dangerous tangle of timber and crazily tilted pilings.

The quarantine ended the city's otyugh-control; several burst up a drainage tunnel here. The mud bottom is difficult terrain; the slick slope takes @Check[type:athletics|dc:12] to scale under pressure.

  • 3× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{Otyugh (4)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori Vaus (10, ally)}
Sewer otyughsTrivial · 30 XP @ L8

3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.kPBSSOuR8n8vZHXB]{otyughs} wallow and lurch out at passersby (apply the Elite adjustment for a meaner fight). If the PCs haven't met @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} yet, she bursts from B3 a round in, shrieking with delight, and joins the fight to earn goodwill.

pages.name: Part 2 — The Emperor of Old Korvosa

text.content:
Part 2. Pilts Swastel — failed impresario of the gore-playhouse Exemplary Execrables — has crowned himself Emperor of Old Korvosa. He rules Old Dock by spectacle: real-death 'plays,' the mob game blood pig, and an ornately carved guillotine (the 'tall knife') with a standing appetite. When volunteers run short, he invents traitors.

Researching the emperor: each attempt takes 1d4 hours and a @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:20] (gather information; substantially harder off-island). Better results name Pilts, his guillotine, his salvaged playhouse hoard, and his newest 'acquisition' — a captive painter.

His 'palace' is a block of converted tenements in central Old Dock. Salvator is there. So is the audience the PCs will need to play to — or fight through.

C. Pilts's Palace — a block of gutted tenements in central Old Dock, linked by rope bridges and rooftop fields, where the Emperor holds court from an hour past sunrise to dusk. The intended path is showmanship, not siege: petition the Emperor, accept the blood pig wager, win an audience with Salvator. Fighting the whole court at once is an Extreme-tier mistake — Extreme · 180 XP @ L8.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C1-C2 — p.152.

The interior of this home has been gutted. A huge mound of rubble-broken timbers, bits of wall, ruined furniture, and other debris-lies heaped in the center of the room. Rickety wooden stairs wind up to a splintered hole in the wall near the roof above.

The attic guardroom beyond holds 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thugs} (−2 to their Perception — they're arguing about whether chokers have skeletons). Rope bridges exit level (NE) and climbing 10 ft (SW, to the throne balcony); the ground-floor stairs are rubble-choked.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thug (5)}
GuardroomLow · 60 XP @ L8

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thugs} — posted only while the alarm is down.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C3 — p.153.

This open-air balcony is shielded from rain and sun by a brightly colored canvas roof that extends up over the area like a half-dome, held in place by a wooden framework. The inside of the canvas has been decorated with scenes of gruesome debauchery; battlefields, executions, torture chambers, and human-eating monsters all vie for space. The balcony contains two major features of note. The first is a high-backed throne that looks like a poor man's version of the Crimson Throne, a thing of blood-red cushions and silks and spikes. Directly west of the throne stands an intimidating device: a tall guillotine of carved wood and bone, its base depicting grasping demonic feet and the housing of its glittering blade a leering demonic face.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.piltsSwastel0001]{Pilts Swastel} holds court here daily with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jabbyrGnome00001]{Jabbyr} (in executioner's costume at the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.tallKnifeGuillo1]{Tall Knife}) and 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{thugs}, hearing mob reports, citizen pleas, and execution dockets.

Negotiating: Pilts happily confirms Salvator is his 'houseguest' but refuses every plea and threat — until boredom suggests the wager (see A Rousing Game). An unnoticed suggestion-style spell can move him; a noticed one means the Tall Knife.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.piltsSwastel0001]{Pilts Swastel (10)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jabbyrGnome00001]{Jabbyr (8)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Thug (5)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.tallKnifeGuillo1]{The Tall Knife (10)}
The full court (DON'T)Extreme · 180 XP @ L8

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.piltsSwastel0001]{Pilts (10)} + @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jabbyrGnome00001]{Jabbyr (8)} + 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{thugs} + a mob of spectators — an Extreme bloodbath best avoided until the blood pig audience isolates Pilts (see the Development under Blood Pig).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C4-C5 — p.154.

The large, flat roof of this long building has been converted into a strange sort of marshaling yard or game field. Two large square areas have been outlined with what could be blood, while at either end to the west and east, small wooden cages have been set up to the side of a hole in the roof that drops into the upper floor of the building below. Flights of stairs lead up from the roof to a balcony to the north and the gently slanted roof of a building to the south.

The blood pig field: cages (C4a/C4e) over trapdoors; wolverine pits (C4b/C4f — one starving @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.6YOyTHRGqkYl2Prb]{wolverine} each); team squares (C4c/C4d). The southern spectator rooftops (C5) hold 4 patrolling @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{thugs} off-game, and dozens of lowlifes during one.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C6 — p.155.

Crates, boxes, and barrels fill this long storeroom, making it difficult to judge the room's actual dimensions.

@Check[type:perception|dc:25] turns up a small jewelry box of worthless costume glass — and a real ring of chameleon power (quirk: activating or removing it turns all the wearer's hair a random unnatural colour; Pilts refused to wear it).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C7 — p.155.

This large room might once have been an attic storage area, but the rafters above now brood over an empty chamber. To the northwest, a large portion of the roof and floor below have collapsed entirely, leaving a void looking out over the sodden skyline of Old Korvosa.

Six @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chokerBrute00001]{choker brutes} — Pilts's corpse-disposal partners since the theater days — nest here. The collapsed gap can be a stealth entry (@Check[type:athletics|dc:20] to climb the 25-ft exterior), but within 5 feet of the broken floor a square crumbles: @Check[type:reflex|dc:15] to lurch back or fall for @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] (and the noise).

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chokerBrute00001]{Choker Brute (5)}
The nestModerate · 90 XP @ L8

6 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.chokerBrute00001]{choker brutes} strike from the rafters — at the door from outside, or 3 rounds after southern intruders start moving. They fight to the death.

@Check[type:perception|dc:15] finds their bag wedged in the SE rafters: ~75 gp re-scaled in coin, a pearl-inset silk glove, a masterwork hand crossbow, and a wand of slow (13 charges).

pages.name: C8-C10. Stairway, Art Supplies & Trophy Hall

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C8-C10 — p.156.

C8, a collapsed stairway, and C9, where Salvator's confiscated paints and his finished commissions are stored. C10 is the trophy hall:

The air in this room smells sickly sweet-a combination of flowers and vinegar. The unpleasant smell likely comes from the fourteen poorly preserved heads mounted on the walls of this grisly trophy hall. Most of the heads are human, although two are those of elves and one is from a dwarf with a beard roughly sheered off to match the cut to the neck. To the north, a small, child-sized bed sits against the wall opposite a wooden table decorated with a magnificent set of silverware.

Pilts talks to the heads on sleepless nights; the small bed is Jabbyr's. The C11 door carries an alarm spell keyed to Pilts.

The table is set with a full silverware service (~50 gp re-scaled) and a platinum-and-crystal brandy decanter (~75 gp).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C11 — p.157.

This extravagantly decorated bedroom would seem to belong in the richest of noble villas or monarchs' castles-at least, until one looks a little more closely at the sheets on the four-poster bed and notes how stained and frayed they are, or examines the tapestries and bed curtains and sees the patches of mold and threadbare edges. A tall, well-stocked bookcase to the south turns out to be leaning against the wall for support; the contents of its sagging shelves are poorly produced books with violent or erotic names on their mildewed spines. Everything is slightly musty, stained with age, and well beyond its prime. Only the three paintings hanging on the wall hold up to closer examination, but their grisly subject matter might make them difficult to show in most public venues. To the north, a simple wooden door is secured with a lock and a heavy wooden bar.

Pilts sleeps and reads here after dark, and for an hour each night unbolts the C12 door to feed Salvator and collect finished paintings.

The three Scream originals (painted before his muse fell silent — and full of buried truth): a shadow-clad figure at a dolmen (@Check[type:religion|dc:25]: Zon-Kuthon entering ancient Nidal; ~45 gp); desert riders over skull-dunes (@Check[type:religion|dc:30]: a scene from the Umbral Leaves; ~110 gp); a man peeling away his own flesh to reveal blue scales (@Check[type:society|dc:30] places the horizon-castle as Scarwall in Belkzen; @Check[type:arcana|dc:30] matches the scales to a blue dragon; ~200 gp). If @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} is along she wants all three, settles for a split — and keeps offering to buy the PCs' share.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.3, C12 — p.158.

The air in this room is an unpleasant mix of body odor and paint. A lumpy straw mattress lies on the floor in one corner of the room, partially covered by a few blankets, while in the other stands a large easel on which rests a nearly completed painting of immense fiends attacking a village.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.salvatorScream01]{Salvator Scream} paints here under lock, bar, and beating. He begs rescue of any visitor who isn't the Emperor — but says nothing of Neolandus while Pilts lives. His price: the Emperor's death, and escort to the mainland (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:10] with a credible rescue promise; Intimidation or magic also work — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} cheerfully recommends both).

His testimony is the chapter's hinge: Ileosa murdered the king; Neolandus knows; the Arkonas have him. On to Part 3 — and the rescue earns the trail to Arkona Palace.

The wager. Pilts offers it when pleading stalls: beat his champion team — the eight-thug Shinglesnipes — at blood pig, and the PCs get five supervised minutes with Salvator (plus, if they surrendered their gear at the door, its return and safe escort from Old Dock). The rules were invented by a madman, are never written down, and change weekly — narrate Pilts declaiming them; confusion is part of the show.

The rules (as declaimed this week): first team to 5 points; a point = your team's pig into your team's pit. No weapons, no spellcasting (each violation = a point to the other team); unarmed Strikes are legal. Up to 8 a side, starting in the marked squares; a fresh pig is craned into the cage opposite the scoring team's pit; a coin flip starts play.

  • Picking up a pig (cage or loose): an Interact action that provokes reactions; a loose pig also needs a successful unarmed Strike vs AC 15 to corner.
  • Carrying a live pig: each round roll d6 on the Squirming Pig table — 1 slump (lose 1 action) · 2 squirm (@Check[type:athletics|dc:18] or drop) · 3 squeal (the crowd roars) · 4 bite (pig attacks +6, 1 damage; if damaged, check to drop) · 5 kick (@Check[type:reflex|dc:18] or drop) · 6 panic (all four at once). Taking damage while carrying: @Check[type:fortitude|dc:18] or drop. Thugs knock their pigs out first.
  • Loose pigs bolt 30 ft from the nearest creature each round (never off the roof or into a pit). Pass = Interact to an adjacent ally; throw = improvised −2 Strike, 10-ft increments; catch @Check[type:athletics|dc:18]; intercept (reaction) @Check[type:athletics|dc:22]; steal = a Disarm attempt, then the table.
  • Scoring: drop into an adjacent pit free, or throw at the 1-ft mouth (a Strike vs AC 10). The pits are 10 ft deep with a starving @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.6YOyTHRGqkYl2Prb]{wolverine} in each — killing a wolverine forfeits for the killer's team; it spends 1d3 rounds devouring each scored piglet, otherwise claws at anything within 5 ft of the mouth.
  • Betting: the thugs give 2-to-1 against the PCs, up to 100 gp per PC.
  • 8× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Shinglesnipe (5)}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2.Actor.6YOyTHRGqkYl2Prb]{Wolverine (2)} (in the pits)
BLOOD PIG — the ShinglesnipesSevere · 120 XP @ L8

8 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.emperorsThug0001]{Emperor's Thugs} (the Shinglesnipes) — but it's a game, not a battle: unarmed, pig-fixated, crowd-roared. Run it fast and absurd.

Development — the sore loser. On a PC win Pilts demands two-of-three and adds @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jabbyrGnome00001]{Jabbyr} to the Shinglesnipes; after a second loss he calls for three-of-five — and is told they're out of pigs. He nearly guillotines the messenger, then laughs, congratulates the PCs, and brings them up the balcony with only Jabbyr and two thugs. The supervised meeting (Salvator silent while Pilts lives) is the PCs' best shot at Pilts away from his mob: Moderate · 110 XP @ L9.

Part 3. Salvator's testimony points across Old Korvosa to Arkona Palace, where both Neolandus and Vencarlo sit in the dungeons below — insurance policies in a rakshasa's game.

The Arkona secret: generations ago, on a Vudra trade voyage, the human Arkonas were slaughtered and replaced by rakshasas. 'Glorio' is the rakshasa Bahor; his 'sister Meliya' is Vimanda (Verik's 'lover' from Chapter 1) — rivals locked in covert sibling war beneath a flawless family front. The palace crawls with lesser rakshasas and magically-thralled human 'relatives' who believe the family merely criminal.
